label: Yellowstone National Park
sub: Gray wolf reintroduction site (1995). 14 wolves released Jan 1995; ~525 wolves in Greater Yellowstone Ecosystem by 2024. Triggered landmark 'trophic cascade' research.

label: Vermilion Cliffs, Arizona
sub: First California condor wild release site (Jan 1996). Six birds released after 9 years of total captivity. Now one of the primary wild condor territories in the Southwest.

label: Doñana National Park, Spain
sub: Last stronghold of Iberian lynx in 2002 (94 individuals total). Site of first captive breeding center (El Acebuche). Population now exceeds 2,021 across Iberian Peninsula.

label: Wolong Nature Reserve, Sichuan
sub: China's premier giant panda research and breeding center. Houses largest captive panda population. Giant panda wild population reached 1,864 in 2015 national survey.

label: North Atlantic Humpback Whale Range
sub: Primary feeding grounds of recovering North Atlantic humpback whale population. Estimated 10,000–12,000 individuals by 2018, up from ~1,200 at population nadir.

label: Codfish Island (Whenua Hou), NZ
sub: Primary kakapo sanctuary. Predator-eradicated offshore island. Population reached record 252 kakapo in 2023, up from 51 in 1995.

label: Bwindi Impenetrable Forest, Uganda
sub: Home to roughly half of the world's mountain gorilla population. UNESCO World Heritage Site. Population exceeded 1,063 individuals across Virunga + Bwindi by 2024.

label: Virunga Massif, DRC/Rwanda/Uganda
sub: Volcanic mountain range spanning three countries. Critical mountain gorilla habitat. Conservation has continued through armed conflict. Virunga NP rangers have died protecting gorillas.

label: Florida Everglades
sub: Core American alligator recovery area. Delisted 1987 after recovering from near-extinction. Estimated 5 million alligators now in southeastern US. Ongoing habitat restoration via CERP.

label: Monterey Bay, California
sub: Central California sea otter recovery zone. ~3,000 southern sea otters along coast by 2015. Otters control sea urchin populations, enabling kelp forest recovery.

label: Arabian Oryx Sanctuary, Oman
sub: First reintroduction site for Arabian oryx, extinct in wild since 1972. UNESCO World Heritage Site. Wild population grew to 1,000+ across Arabian Peninsula, enabling IUCN downgrade from 'Extinct in Wild' to 'Vulnerable' in 2011.

label: Sundarbans, Bangladesh/India
sub: World's largest mangrove forest. Vital Bengal tiger habitat. India's tiger population reached 3,167 in 2023 national census — 75%+ of global wild tiger population.

label: Upper Gulf of California — Vaquita Habitat
sub: Last remaining habitat of vaquita porpoise, world's most endangered marine mammal. Fewer than 20 individuals remain. Devastated by illegal gillnets targeting totoaba fish.

label: Kanha Tiger Reserve, India
sub: One of India's premier tiger reserves. Also critical for barasingha (swamp deer) recovery. Inspired Rudyard Kipling's Jungle Book. Part of Project Tiger network covering 75,000+ km².

label: San Diego Zoo, California
sub: Primary California condor captive breeding facility since 1982. Hatched hundreds of condors for wild reintroduction. Houses backup population of ~190 captive condors.

label: Lamar Valley, Yellowstone
sub: Primary gray wolf viewing and monitoring area. Known as 'America's Serengeti.' Home to multiple wolf packs. Site of key trophic cascade research on elk overgrazing and river restoration.

label: Scottish Highlands
sub: Red kite (Milvus milvus) reintroduction site (1989). Extinct in England and Scotland for centuries. Over 4,600 breeding pairs now in UK as a result of successive reintroductions. One of UK's most successful rewilding programs.

label: Pantanal, Brazil
sub: World's largest tropical wetland. Core of jaguar range recovery. Rewilding Argentina has reintroduced jaguars from captive breeding. Jaguar populations stable at ~170,000 across range but declining in southern portions.

label: Chesapeake Bay, Maryland/Virginia
sub: Critical bald eagle nesting and wintering area. Population collapsed from DDT eggshell thinning; nesting pairs recovered 17,000% after DDT ban and ESA protection. 71,467 nesting pairs in US by 2021.

label: Danube Delta, Romania
sub: UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and Ramsar site. Europe's largest river delta. Key breeding ground for Dalmatian pelicans. Ongoing EU LIFE-funded rehabilitation of Danube floodplains and sturgeon populations.
